SAMPLE SLIDE DECK An UCE Workshop by Dan Swart “Be the Ball, Danny…Be the Ball” (updated: 2023-12-17) Today's workshop is brought to you by the letter "U" "Unique!" 2 WHY ARE WE HERE TODAY? 3 To
LEARN 4 to have FUN 5 And, to
MAKE A
DIFFERENCE! 6 This workshop is designed
specifically to help you succeed; so you can make a difference 7 About me:
Dan Swart I help owners use their companies to create prosperity and fulfillment for themselves and others. A 'recovering tax CPA' who loves entrepreneurs way more than tax issues. I now teach companies to be even more Unique, Capable, and Effective 8 How to contact me: (916) 802-1040 dan@danswart.com 9 My Mission is to Create Happy Customers How About You? 10 THE CHALLENGE WE ALL
FACE... 11 How to be Unique in the marketplace] 12 How to be Unique in the marketplace] Because the more Unique, the more success. 12 How to be Unique in the marketplace] Because the more Unique, the more success. Being different is more important than you think! 12 In the Marketplace Contrast is Power 13 The very best way to contrast with your competitors 14 The very best way to contrast with your competitors is to be very Effective AND very Capable 14 The very best way to contrast with your competitors is to be very Effective AND very Capable from you customer's point of view 14 Unique means Standing out in a crowd 15 Unique means being unconventional 16 Avoid conventional logic because all your
competitors will use that 17 Avoid conventional logic because all your
competitors will use that Test counter-intuitive ideas because your competitors won't 17 Why This Works... 18 The unstoppable chain reaction: 19 The unstoppable chain reaction: Become Effective, then 19 The unstoppable chain reaction: Become Effective, then Build your Capability to deliver 19 The unstoppable chain reaction: Become Effective, then Build your Capability to deliver And you will be irresistably UNIQUE 19 So, what does it mean to be "effective?" 20 Effective
means knowing what creates
REAL value for customers - in
their terms and having the capability to
deliver it upon demand 21 Effective means... 22 Effective means... Strictly from the customer's point of view 22 Your efforts must be Useful, or Fulfill a desire, or Solve a problem 23 Effective
does NOT mean Expedient 24 Expedient = Quick, Interim or Temporary Short-term, often at the expense of the long-term Putting out fires, often at the expense of the
customer 25 Strangely, the opposite is NOT true: Efficient ≠ Effective 26 Strangely, the opposite is NOT true: Efficient ≠ Effective
As you become more effective you will automatically become more efficient 26 Productive ≠ Effective 27 Productive ≠ Effective
As you become more effective you will automatically
become more productive 27 But, don't just take my word for it... 28 But, don't just take my word for it...
Let's hear what Warren Buffet thinks about effectiveness in delighting customers Warren Buffet on Delighting Customers 30 sec 28 Some Cold, Hard Facts... 29 Half of new businesses fail in 5 years. 30 Half of new businesses fail in 5 years. By year 10 fully two-thirds are gone. 30 Half of new businesses fail in 5 years. By year 10 fully two-thirds are gone. Only 10% make it to 20 years. 30 Studies and surveys also show shocking failure rates
for improvement initiatives that can range from 50% to 70% or even higher. 31 Companies that fail are not:
* Unique enough,
* Capable enough, or
* Effective enough from the customer's point of view 32 If you're looking for a 'sure-fire', low-cost way to prosper in the marketplace... 33 If you're looking for a 'sure-fire', low-cost way to prosper in the marketplace... Become very Unique by becoming very Effective and very Capable 33 It’s not remarkable that companies fail. 34 It’s not remarkable that companies fail. It’s remarkable that they fail so often, and have for so long 34 Obviously, we make the same mistakes over and over again. 35 Obviously, we make the same mistakes over and over again. Otherwise, those grim survival statistics would have improved long ago, RIGHT? 35 And what about the
companies that are just
surviving? They are neither fully
alive nor completely
dead 36 This is not a laziness problem, or an intelligence problem. 37 This is not a laziness problem, or an intelligence problem. Most business owners are very hard-working, and good at what they do. 37 This is not a laziness problem, or an intelligence problem. Most business owners are very hard-working, and good at what they do. Wouldn’t you agree? 37 But they’re not very good at turning all that hard work into prosperity and a better quality of life are they? 38 Where Companies Go Wrong 39 What?! 40 They are ‘Same-As’: EACH COMPANY JUST
LIKE THE REST Afraid to be different 41 'Same-As' companies don't stand a chance in the marketplace 42 They Copy, Copy, Copy
Everyone from business schools
to self-help books continue to
tell owners that copying others is
the solution to their problems. 43 'Follow-the-Herd' companies don't stand a chance in the marketplace either 44 Woo Hoo! Let's do what everyone else does! 45 On top of that, most 'Solutions' are Incompatible with their existing systems 46 For example, companies naturally adopt the style of
management known as ‘command-and-control’ 47 Unfortunately, most improvement
schemes (and many
company goals) are
incompatible with this
style of management 48 So, most improvement efforts are doomed from the outset ] 49 So, what's a leader to do? 50 So, what's a leader to do? * Be even more Effective, 50 So, what's a leader to do? * Be even more Effective,
* even more Capable, and 50 So, what's a leader to do? * Be even more Effective,
* even more Capable, and
* and become ever more Unique 50 So, what's a leader to do? * Be even more Effective,
* even more Capable, and
* and become ever more Unique from your customer's point of view. 50 These things hardly cost a thing... 51 These things hardly cost a thing... * But, they require a different mindset 51 These things hardly cost a thing... * But, they require a different mindset
* A sense of urgency, and 51 These things hardly cost a thing... * But, they require a different mindset
* A sense of urgency, and
* The willingness to try new things 51 The reward is to become
Unique in the marketplace 52 Which is the Point! 53 WHAT TO DO NOW? 54 FIRST... 55 FIRST...
1 - Adopt a simple, straightforward aim such as "Our
Mission is to Create Happy Customers!" 55 FIRST...
1 - Adopt a simple, straightforward aim such as "Our
Mission is to Create Happy Customers!"
Lose the corporate-speak, gobbelty-gook jargon. It
makes it MUCH HARDER for everyone to do a great job. 55 Next... Learn
first-hand what
customers value -- from
their point of view 56 Next... Learn
first-hand, what customers
experience when dealing with
your company (the good, bad, and ugly) 57 Remember, management Is Not Very Good at Guessing
What Customers Value 58 Remember, management Is Not Very Good at Guessing
What Customers Value So, it PAYS (a lot) to Ask Them Directly 58 Case Study
Initially, Dr. T and her team came up with 23 Ideas of creating value for her customers 59 Let's see how they did... 60 61 62 63 64 If there is any way at all 65 If there is any way at all Ask Customers Directly 65 Then...
create Capability so
customers can pull what
they value from you
when, where, and how
they want to 66 Case Study
Management at Stew Leonard's have mastered the Capability to 'Listen, then Deliver' Power of Customer Suggestions 7 min 67 SOME PRINCIPLES for Action 68 Sound PRINCIPLES applied daily get AMAZING RESULTS 69 PRINCIPLE: Focus on measures of Effectiveness Not on measures of Efficiency 70 PRINCIPLE: Focus on measures of Effectiveness Not measures of Productivity 71 To be UNIQUE
Be EFFECTIVE! 72 To be UNIQUE
Be EFFECTIVE! (EVERY DAY) 72 To be UNIQUE
Be CAPABLE! 73 To be UNIQUE
Be CAPABLE! (EVERY DAY) 73 Some Things To AVOID... 74 BEWARE OF... 75 BEWARE OF...
Slogans, and so-called ‘best practices’, 75 BEWARE OF...
Slogans, and so-called ‘best practices’,
Agile, Lean, or Six-Sigma, 75 BEWARE OF...
Slogans, and so-called ‘best practices’,
Agile, Lean, or Six-Sigma,
Mission & Vision statements full of buzzwords, 75 BEWARE OF...
Slogans, and so-called ‘best practices’,
Agile, Lean, or Six-Sigma,
Mission & Vision statements full of buzzwords,
Servant leadership and other such management fads 75 Those only dig deeper the pit you are in 76 THANK YOU! Q&A 77 78
